Don't change any content. Incomplete example is acceptable.

Discuss with me to improve the specification.

# Lex - Vietnamese Telex input method

The specification of the Lex program, a Vietnamese Telex input method.

## Vietnamese Telex Rules

### Diacritics

Diacritics let a character has many variant. We only focus on character diacritics in this section.
Tones have seperated section.

#### Empty

- Any literal alphabet character has empty diacritic by default.
- Only a specific number of characters have other diacritic and it must follow the rules for those
  characters.

#### Circumflex

- Valid for: `A` (Г‚), `E` (ГЉ), `O` (Г”) and their lower cases.

#### Breve

- Valid for: `A` (Д‚) and its lower case.

#### Horn

- Valid for: `O` (Ж ), `U` (ЖЇ) and their lower cases.

#### Stroke

- Valid for: `D` (Дђ) and its lower case.

#### Apply rules

- Only apply diacritic when the immediate previous character doesn't have any diacritic (empty).
- _Circumflex_, _stroke_ are triggered when user inputs the same character (ignore case) with the
  existing character when that character is valid for the applying diacritic.
  - `Aa` -> `Г‚`, `Ee` -> `ГЉ`, `Oo` -> `Г”`.
  - `Dd` -> `Дђ`.
- _Breve_, _horn_ are triggered when user input `W`, `w` after the character when that character is
  valid for the applying diacritic. Otherwise, treat `W`, `w` literally.
  - `Aw` -> `Д‚`.
  - `Ow` -> `Ж `, `Uw` -> `ЖЇ`.
- The existing character keep its case, tone.
- Treat out of scope characters as literal.

#### Override rules

- New valid diacritic can override existing valid diacritic of the same base character.
  - `Д‚a` -> `Г‚`, `Г‚w` -> `Д‚`.
  - `Г”w` -> `Ж `, `Ж o` -> `Г”`.
- Only `A`, `O` have override rules because they have more than one possible character diacritic.
- Keep tone untouched.
- The existing character keep its case, tone.
- Treat out of scope characters as literal.

#### Cancel rules

- The cancellation is triggered if and only if user inputs the character (ignore case) that would
  produce the existing diacritic for the same base character of the immediate previous character.
- The existing character keeps its case, tone, but the diacritic is removed.
  - Circumflex: `Г‚a` -> `Aa`, `б»ђo` -> `Г“o`... and so on for other cases of the same kind.
  - Breve: `Д‚w` -> `Aw`.
  - Horn: `ЖЇw` -> `Uw`... and so on for other cases of the same kind.
  - Stroke: `Дђd` -> `Dd`, `Д‘D` -> `dD`.
- The new character is literal, keeps its case, no tone.
- Switch to literal from the new character position and the following characters. Literal input
  remains until the process is reset (usually when starting new word).
  - Existing `Г‚`, input `aa`, result `Aaa`.
- When the user backspaces pass before the literal point, we switch to Vietnamese input again.

#### Auto-fill rules

- The auto-fill is only applied for `Ж°ЖЎ` syllable immediately before the user input, when one of the
  character in the syllable is missing horn: `uЖЎ` and `Ж°o`.
- Fill the missing _horn_ when the input is one of these character (ignore case): `C`, `I`, `M`,
  `N`, `P`, `T`, `U` and their lower cases.
  - Fill missing horn on `U`: `uЖЎn` -> `Ж°ЖЎn`.
  - Fill missing horn on `O`: `Ж°Гіp` -> `Ж°б»›p`.
- The new input character is appended literally.
- Tone and case of existing characters are preserved on the original characters.

### Tones

Tones let a word has many variants, each variant has different meaning. Only the following
Vietnamese vowel could have tones: `A`, `E`, `I`, `O`, `U`, `Y` and their corresponding variants.
These vowel could take any tone.

#### Level (ngang)

- The default tone, it's the vowel without any tone mark.
- When a vowel has other tone mark, it can be reset to level by input `Z`, `z`.
- `Z`, `z` are the input to reset the tone to level.
- If no tone to clear, `Z`, `z` will be appended literally. Switch to literal from `Z`, `z` position
  and the following characters. Literal input remains until the process is reset (usually when
  starting new word).

#### Rising (sбєЇc)

- Example of rising tone: `ГЃ`, `бєѕ`, `б»љ`...and all other corresponding variants.
- Use `S`, `s` to put the rising tone to the Vietnamese vowels, e.g. `As` -> `ГЃ`, `ГЉs` -> `бєѕ`, `Ж s`
  -> `б»љ`.

#### Falling (huyб»Ѓn)

- Example of falling tone: `ГЂ`, `б»Ђ`, `б»њ`...and all other corresponding variants.
- Use `F`, `f` to put the falling tone to the Vietnamese vowels, e.g. `Af` -> `ГЂ`, `ГЉf` -> `б»Ђ`, `Ж f`
  -> `б»њ`.

#### Dipping-rising (hб»Џi)

- Example of dipping-rising tone: `бєў`, `б»‚`, `б»ћ`...and all other corresponding variants.
- Use `R`, `r` to put the dipping-rising tone to the Vietnamese vowels, e.g. `Ar` -> `бєў`, `ГЉr` ->
  `б»‚`, `Ж r` -> `б»ћ`.

#### Rising-glottalized (ngГЈ)

- Example of rising-glottalized tone: `Гѓ`, `б»„`, `б» `...and all other corresponding variants.
- Use `X`, `x` to put the rising-glottalized tone to the Vietnamese vowels, e.g. `Ax` -> `Гѓ`, `ГЉx`
  -> `б»„`, `Ж x` -> `б» `.

#### Falling-glottalized (nбє·ng)

- Example of falling-glottalized tone: `бє `, `б»†`, `б»ў`...and all other corresponding variants.
- Use `J`, `j` to put the falling-glottalized tone to the Vietnamese vowels, e.g. `Aj` -> `бє `, `ГЉj`
  -> `б»†`, `Ж j` -> `б»ў`.

#### Tone position rules

The process of applying tone to a word go through many steps:

- The process starts when user inputs trigger character: `Z`, `S`, `F`, `R`, `X`, `J` and their
  lower cases.
- Iterate backward from the last existing character (the new input is the tone trigger character).
- If a vowel is found first, continue go backward until hitting the first consonant or hit the first
  character, then stop.
- If a consonant is found first, continue go backword until found the first vowel, then continue go
  backward until hitting the first consonant or the first character, then stop.
- After iterated, a context have been formed either `<consonants>`, `<vowels><consonants>`,
  `<consonant><vowels><consonants>`, `<vowels>` or `<consonant><vowels>`.
- Focus on the `<vowels>`, it could be one vowel but could be multiple vowels, the tone will be
  applied on one of these vowels.
- If a different tone other than the new tone existed, reset the existing tone in the vowels.
- If the existing tone is the same with the new tone (including level), reset the existing tone in
  the vowels and append the new character (trigger character) literally. Switch to literal from the
  new character position and the following characters. Literal input remains until the process is
  reset (usually when starting new word).
- Put the new tone corresponding to the trigger character, the position based on the following
  rules.
- Exceptions:
  - Word exactly `QU` (and their lower cases), could not put tone, this is a consonant only.
- If one vowel, put the tone on that vowel.
- If multiple vowels, follow these rules, priority matter:
  - Put on `Ж ` (and its lower case) if existed.
  - Put on `ГЉ`, `Г‚`, `Г”`, `Д‚`, `ЖЇ` (and their lower cases, priority matter), if existed.
  - Word start with `GI`, `QU` (and their lower cases) and has more vowels, put on next vowel, `I` /
    `U` count as part of the consonant.
  - Vowels exactly `OA`, `OE`, `OO`, `UY` (and their lower cases), put on first vowel.
  - Start with `OA`, `OE`, `OO`, `UY` (and their lower cases), put on second vowel.
  - Otherwise, put on first vowel.
- Preserve case and diacritic on all vowels.
- Append the trigger character as literal character when no tone can be applied.

content.text:
No content changed. I reviewed only the Tones section in [`specs/lex-vietnamese-telex-input-method.md`](file:///<redacted>/lex/specs/lex-vietnamese-telex-input-method.md#L84-L163).

## Main gaps / contradictions

### 1. Tone trigger behavior is split between вЂњdescriptionвЂќ and вЂњposition rulesвЂќ

Each tone description says `S/F/R/X/J` вЂњputвЂќ a tone on Vietnamese vowels, while `Z` resets to level. The actual cancellation/literal behavior is only described later in the tone position rules.

Potential issue: readers may infer from the individual tone sections that:

- `As` always becomes `ГЃ`.
- `ГЃs` maybe remains `ГЃ` or becomes something else.

But the apply rule later says same tone input resets and appends literal:

- `ГЃs` should become `As`.
- `ГЂf` should become `Af`.
- `Az` should become `Az` and switch to literal.

This is probably intended, but the per-tone sections do not mention that tone triggers also cancel the same tone.

Suggested improvement: in each non-level tone section, explicitly say the trigger applies that tone according to вЂњTone position rulesвЂќ, including cancellation when the same tone already exists.

---

### 2. `Z` behavior overlaps with generic вЂњsame toneвЂќ rule

[`Level`](file:///<redacted>/lex/specs/lex-vietnamese-telex-input-method.md#L90-L97) says:

- `Z/z` resets existing tone to level.
- If no tone to clear, append `Z/z` literally and switch to literal.

Later, [`Tone position rules`](file:///<redacted>/lex/specs/lex-vietnamese-telex-input-method.md#L144-L148) say:

- If existing tone is the same as the new tone, including level, reset existing tone and append the trigger literally.

These are compatible if вЂњno toneвЂќ means вЂњlevel toneвЂќ, but the spec should make that equivalence explicit.

Potential ambiguity:

- Is level an actual tone state, or absence of tone?
- When `a` receives `z`, is that вЂњsame tone levelвЂќ or вЂњno tone to clearвЂќ?

Suggested improvement: define a model such as:

> Internally, every vowel has exactly one tone state: level, rising, falling, dipping-rising, rising-glottalized, or falling-glottalized. Level has no visible tone mark.

Then `Z` becomes a normal tone trigger for the level tone.

---

### 3. Order of reset vs. position selection is ambiguous

The rules currently say:

1. Focus on `<vowels>`.
2. Reset existing different tone in the vowels.
3. Put the new tone based on position rules.

This works for most cases, but position selection may depend on the tone-stripped or original vowel form.

Example ambiguity:

- Existing: `hГіa`
- Input: `f`
- Should result be `hГІa`.

Probably yes.

But if the existing tone is on a vowel that is not the newly selected vowel, the spec should make clear that tone position is recomputed from the full vowel cluster after clearing the previous tone, not simply replaced in-place.

Suggested improvement:

> Tone replacement always clears any existing tone in the selected vowel cluster first, then recomputes the tone target from the clusterвЂ™s diacritics and spelling rules.

---

### 4. вЂњExisting tone in the vowelsвЂќ assumes only one tone mark

[`Line 144`](file:///<redacted>/lex/specs/lex-vietnamese-telex-input-method.md#L144) says reset the existing tone in the vowels.

Gap: It does not state whether malformed input with multiple tone marks is possible or how to handle it.

Examples from pasted/pre-existing text:

- `ГЎГЁ`
- `бєЇб»™`
- `hГіГ `

Possible policies:

1. Normalize all vowels in the cluster to level, then apply the new tone.
2. Only reset the first/last tone found.
3. Treat malformed cluster as literal / no apply.

Suggested improvement: state invariant or behavior:

> A valid Vietnamese input state should contain at most one tone mark in the active vowel cluster. If multiple are found, all are cleared before applying the new tone.

Or if malformed text is out of scope, state that.

---

### 5. Word-boundary / reset boundary is not defined in tone scanning

The algorithm says iterate backward until consonants/vowels are found, but does not define how spaces, punctuation, numbers, symbols, or previous literal-mode boundaries interact.

Examples:

- `a-s`
- `a.s`
- `a s`
- `abc123s`
- `a_s`
- after literal switch: `Asxyz`

Should `s` apply across punctuation or stop at punctuation? Probably it should stop at word boundary.

Suggested improvement: define вЂњwordвЂќ / вЂњactive contextвЂќ boundary:

> Tone scanning only considers the current alphabetic Vietnamese word since the last reset/literal boundary. Spaces, punctuation, numbers, and explicit literal-mode boundaries stop scanning.

---

### 6. вЂњConsonantвЂќ is not formally defined

[`Lines 136-141`](file:///<redacted>/lex/specs/lex-vietnamese-telex-input-method.md#L136-L141) use вЂњvowelвЂќ and вЂњconsonantвЂќ. The vowel set is defined earlier, but consonants are not.

Potential ambiguity:

- Is `Дђ` a consonant?
- Are `W`, `Z`, `F`, etc. consonants before they become trigger/literal?
- Are non-Vietnamese alphabet characters consonants or boundaries?

Suggested improvement:

> A consonant is an alphabetic character that is not a Vietnamese vowel variant. Non-alphabetic characters are word boundaries, not consonants.

If that is intended.

---

### 7. `QU` exception conflicts with later `QU` special handling unless scoped carefully

The exception says:

- Word exactly `QU` cannot put tone.

Later:

- Word starts with `QU` and has more vowels, `U` counts as part of the consonant.

These are likely compatible:

- `qu` + `s` => `qus` literal because no vowel target.
- `qua` + `s` => `quГЎ`.

But the wording вЂњWord exactly `QU`вЂќ should include case combinations and diacritic/tone state assumptions.

Potential ambiguity:

- `QГє`?
- `QГ№`?
- `qU`?
- `qЖ°`?

Suggested improvement: say:

> For tone placement, an initial `qu` sequence treats `u` as consonantal only when followed by another vowel in the same word. The bare word `qu` has no tone target.

This may remove the need for a separate exception.

---

### 8. `GI` special case may be too broad

Rule:

> Word start with `GI` and has more vowels, put on next vowel, `I` counts as part of the consonant.

This is correct for many words:

- `gia` -> `giГЎ`
- `gieng` -> likely `giбєїng` depending spelling/diacritic input.

But вЂњword starts with `GI`вЂќ may incorrectly apply to words where `i` is the actual vowel, especially if there is no following vowel. The rule says вЂњand has more vowelsвЂќ, so maybe okay.

Potential ambiguity:

- `gГ¬` from `gif`: There is only `i`, so tone should be on `i`.
- `gi` + `f` should become `gГ¬`, not `gif`.
- `giau` + `s` should place on which vowel? `giГЎu`? `giбєҐu` requires `Гў` and different cluster logic.

Suggested improvement: clarify:

> Initial `gi` treats `i` as consonantal only if another vowel follows immediately in the same vowel cluster. Otherwise `i` remains the tone target.

---

### 9. Multi-vowel placement priority has possible contradictions

Rules for multiple vowels:

1. Put on `Ж `.
2. Put on `ГЉ`, `Г‚`, `Г”`, `Д‚`, `ЖЇ`, priority order.
3. Initial `GI`, `QU` special case.
4. Exact `OA`, `OE`, `OO`, `UY` => first vowel.
5. Starts with `OA`, `OE`, `OO`, `UY` => second vowel.
6. Otherwise first vowel.

Because вЂњpriority mattersвЂќ, earlier rules override later ones.

Potential issue: `QU`/`GI` special handling comes after diacritic priority. That means if the next vowel after `qu`/`gi` has a prioritized diacritic, behavior is probably still fine, but if the consonantal `u/i` itself has a diacritic somehow, priority may pick it.

Examples to decide:

- `quЖЎs`: Should tone be on `ЖЎ`, probably `quб»›`.
- `qЖ°б»›`: malformed maybe.
- `giГЄs`: Should be `giбєї`? likely yes.
- `giЖ°s`: Should be `giб»©`? likely yes.

More important: if `QU` comes before `UY`-style rules, words like `quy` are tricky.

- `quys` вЂ” should this be `quГЅs`, `qГєy`, or literal?
  Vietnamese word вЂњquГЅвЂќ has tone on `y`, treating `u` as consonantal.
- Current algorithm:
  - vowels are `U`,`Y`
  - multiple vowels
  - no special diacritic
  - word starts with `QU` and has more vowels => put on next vowel
  - result `quГЅ`
  Good.

But if `UY` exact rule came before `QU`, it would put on `U`, so current order is important. The spec should explicitly say these examples.

Suggested improvement: add examples for `quy`, `qua`, `gia`, `gi`, because they validate the special-case order.

---

### 10. `OA/OE/OO/UY` exact vs. prefix rules need sharper definition

Rules:

- Vowels exactly `OA`, `OE`, `OO`, `UY`, put on first vowel.
- Start with `OA`, `OE`, `OO`, `UY`, put on second vowel.

This distinction is subtle and likely intentional:

- `oa` -> `ГІa`
- `oan`? Vowels are still `oa`, consonant follows, so вЂњvowels exactly `OA`вЂќ applies, producing `oГ n`.
- `oai` has vowel cluster `oai`, starts with `OA`, so tone on `a`: `oГЎi`.

Potential ambiguity: вЂњVowels exactlyвЂќ means the vowel cluster, not the whole word. That should be explicit.

Also `OO` is unusual. Need examples:

- `oo` -> tone first?
- `oong` -> if vowel cluster is `oo`, exact `OO` still first?
- `ooo` -> starts with `OO`, second?

Suggested improvement: use вЂњvowel cluster exactlyвЂќ and вЂњvowel cluster starts withвЂќ.

---

### 11. Priority list may produce questionable Vietnamese placement for some clusters

The current priority says if multiple vowels and one is `ЖЇ`, put tone on `ЖЇ` before considering `OA/OE/OO/UY`.

Examples:

- `uy` should be handled by exact `UY`, first vowel: `Гєy`.
- But in normal Vietnamese, `quy` should be `quГЅ` due to `qu`.
- `uys` alone under exact `UY` becomes `Гєy`.

For `Ж°ЖЎ`, priority `Ж ` before `ЖЇ` gives `Ж°б»›`, which is correct.

For `Ж°a`, priority `ЖЇ` gives `б»©a`; likely correct.

For `uya`, starts with `UY`, second vowel gives `uГЅa`; maybe intended.

This may be okay, but the spec should define whether the rules are based on modern Vietnamese orthography or a particular input method behavior. Some Telex engines differ on `oa`, `oe`, `uy`.

---

### 12. Literal-mode behavior for tone cancellation should mention backspace

Tone level section and same-tone rule say switch to literal mode, but unlike diacritic cancel rules, the tones section does not say how backspace exits literal mode.

Diacritic cancel rules include:

> When the user backspaces pass before the literal point, we switch to Vietnamese input again.

Tone rules should probably mirror that.

Suggested improvement: add the same backspace/literal-point rule in tone section, or define literal mode once globally.

---

### 13. Case preservation is mostly clear but examples do not cover trigger case

Rule says preserve case and diacritic on all vowels. Good.

Gap: trigger case behavior for literal append is not explicitly repeated for tones.

Examples:

- `ГЃS` -> should be `AS`?
- `ГЎs` -> `as`
- `aZ` -> `aZ`
- `ГЃz` -> `A`

The spec likely means the trigger character is appended literally with its input case when cancellation occurs. It says вЂњappend the new character (trigger character) literallyвЂќ, so this is implied, but examples would remove ambiguity.

---

### 14. вЂњAppend trigger as literal when no tone can be appliedвЂќ may conflict with `Z` literal-mode rule

Final rule:

> Append the trigger character as literal character when no tone can be applied.

Level section says if `Z` has no tone to clear, append literal and switch to literal mode.

Question: For other triggers when no vowel exists, should they also switch to literal mode?

Examples:

- `b` + `s` -> `bs`, and literal mode starts?
- `qu` + `s` -> `qus`, and literal mode starts?
- `123s` -> `123s`, and literal mode starts?

Current final rule does not say switch to literal mode; same-tone cancellation does. This is a behavioral gap.

Suggested decision:

- If no tone can be applied because no vowel target exists, probably append trigger literally but do **not** necessarily switch to literal mode.
- If the trigger cancels an existing same tone or `Z` hits level, switch to literal mode to support intentional literal trigger input.

But the spec should say this explicitly.

---

## Suggested compact model

To reduce ambiguity, I think the section would benefit from defining tone application as a small state machine:

1. Each vowel has:
   - base vowel: `A/E/I/O/U/Y`
   - optional character diacritic: circumflex/breve/horn
   - tone: level/rising/falling/dipping-rising/rising-glottalized/falling-glottalized

2. On `S/F/R/X/J/Z`:
   - Find active word boundary.
   - Find target vowel cluster.
   - If no target vowel exists: append trigger literally.
   - Determine existing cluster tone:
     - If same as requested tone: clear to level, append trigger literally, enter literal mode.
     - Else: clear existing tone, select target vowel by placement rules, apply requested tone.
   - For requested level tone (`Z`), applying means only clearing existing tone.

3. Tone placement uses the tone-stripped vowel cluster and priority list.

This would make `Z`, replacement, cancellation, and placement all consistent.
